storyContext.name: frontend-v1

metadata.sizeNote: Large story — SM may split into 4 sub-stories (see story spec, "Sub-story suggestion" section).

storyContext.problem: Epic 1 shipped a headless HTTP API. Employees and admins need a browser interface to consume the same endpoints without touching the backend. This story delivers a minimal React + Vite + TypeScript SPA covering the five backend surfaces already in production.

policy.rule: Frontend is decoupled: no changes to pom.xml, Java sources, or application.properties.
State management is useState + useEffect only. No Redux / Zustand / TanStack Query.
HTTP client is native fetch. No Axios.
Routing is react-router-dom. Exactly three routes: /, /orders, /admin.
Styling: pick ONE of vanilla CSS, Tailwind, or a small component library. Do not mix.
Design captured in frontend/DESIGN.md BEFORE component code is written.
X-Admin: true header is sent ONLY for requests fired from the /admin route.
X-User-Id header is read from localStorage-persisted "Sign in as" input.
Error display shows ApiError.message, never ApiError.code. Non-2xx responses are never silently swallowed.

Design step (AC 2.1.10) is performed with the Claude Code `frontend-design` skill. The skill proposes layout + component breakdown; participant iterates and commits the agreed outcome to frontend/DESIGN.md BEFORE any component code is written.

ubiquitousLanguage.term: Single-Page Application — one HTML bundle served by Vite dev server (5173) in dev.
React component mapped to a URL path via react-router-dom.
X-User-Id: <employee-id> — read from a "Sign in as" input persisted to localStorage.
X-Admin: true — sent only from the /admin route.
frontend/DESIGN.md — one-page wireframes + design decisions, agreed before implementation.
Backend error response body: { code: string, message: string }. Frontend displays message.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Scaffold frontend/ via npm create vite@latest frontend -- --template react-ts (defaults accepted).
cd frontend && npm install && npm run dev boots Vite dev server on port 5173, zero errors.
Menu view (/) lists today's menu from GET /api/v1/menu; each item shows name, priceChf formatted as CHF, and an "available" indicator.
Order form (on /) picks menu item + quantity (1–10), submits via POST /api/v1/orders with X-User-Id header.
My orders view (/orders) lists caller's orders from GET /api/v1/orders/me sorted newest first, shows item name, quantity, status, and Cancel button next to each SUBMITTED order.
Cancel action calls PATCH /api/v1/orders/{id}/cancel, refreshes list, shows brief confirmation on success.
Admin add-item view (/admin): form for name + priceChf, POSTs to /api/v1/menu/items with X-Admin: true.
Identity handling: X-User-Id read from "Sign in as" input persisted to localStorage; X-Admin: true sent ONLY from /admin route.
Error display: renders ApiError.message (never .code) on non-2xx; never silently swallows a non-2xx response.
frontend/DESIGN.md (one page) summarises agreed wireframes and design decisions BEFORE component code is written.

file.reason: Existing CORS config already permits http://localhost:5173 — no backend change needed
Vite scaffold root (created by npm create vite command)
One-page wireframes + design decisions (AC 2.1.10, must exist BEFORE component code)
X-User-Id / X-Admin header handling + localStorage read/write (AC 2.1.8)

implementationOutline.step: Design step first: invoke the Claude Code `frontend-design` skill; iterate on the skill's proposed component breakdown and layout; commit the agreed outcome to frontend/DESIGN.md. Do NOT skip the skill — this is the ELCA-standard entry point. (AC 2.1.10, policy P10)
Scaffold frontend/ with npm create vite@latest --template react-ts. (AC 2.1.1)
Install react-router-dom. Add App.tsx with three routes (/, /orders, /admin).
Implement auth/identity.ts — read/write X-User-Id in localStorage; expose helper for /admin route to add X-Admin. (AC 2.1.8)
Implement api/menu.ts and api/orders.ts as thin fetch wrappers. Every wrapper throws or returns { data, error } so ErrorBanner can display .message. (AC 2.1.9)
Build MenuPage.tsx: menu list + order form. Wire GET /api/v1/menu and POST /api/v1/orders. (AC 2.1.3, 2.1.4)
Build MyOrdersPage.tsx: list + Cancel action. Wire GET /api/v1/orders/me and PATCH /api/v1/orders/{id}/cancel. (AC 2.1.5, 2.1.6)
Build AdminPage.tsx: name + priceChf form. Wire POST /api/v1/menu/items with X-Admin: true. (AC 2.1.7)
Add ErrorBanner and wire it into each page's error state. (AC 2.1.9)
Add Vitest config + one component test (MenuPage.test.tsx covering menu list render). (DoD)
Verify: npm run dev boots on 5173, npm run build succeeds, npm run lint passes.
Manual smoke: backend + Vite up simultaneously; walk through all 10 ACs; capture screenshots for PR.

test.case: MenuPage renders items from a mocked GET /api/v1/menu response
Vite react-ts default ESLint config passes on all committed frontend/ code
Backend + Vite dev server up simultaneously; walk through all 10 ACs; capture screenshots for PR

definitionOfDone.item: PR description references the frontend-design skill session that produced DESIGN.md (name the skill and paste one representative excerpt or screenshot; makes the tool-use reviewable, not just the artefact)
Manual smoke walkthrough with screenshots attached to PR
frontend/DESIGN.md exists and reflects agreed wireframes
No changes to pom.xml, backend Java sources, or application.properties

deviationsFromELCAi.deviation: No Jira ticket / no Analyzed transition — workshop repo does not use Jira.
No sprint-status.yaml bootstrap — orphan artefact without a board.
No tech-spec-epic-2.md — flat docs/tech-spec.md covers the backend only; frontend is API-consumer.
Base branch is main, not develop — workshop is trunk-based.
SonarQube guardrails not applicable — TypeScript/React project uses ESLint as the analog.
